content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Kelly C. Fincher 
Chief Deputy City Attorney 
City of Merced 
678 West 18th Street 
Merced, California 95340 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-051 
Dear Ms. Fincher: 
April 12, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Merced City Councilmember 
Michael Belluomini regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 
"Act"). 1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section 1090. 
QUESTION 
Under the Act, may Councilmember Belluomini participate in discussions and decisions 
concerning the potential annexation and rezoning of a parcel of land that is owned by a trust 
controlled by a relative of the councilmember's wife? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. Councilman Belluomini does not have a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act 
prohibiting him from participating in decisions concerning the potential annexation and rezoning. 
FACTS 
You are the city attorney for the City of Merced and your write on behalf of Councilmember 
Belluomini. Mr. Belluomini would like to propose an annexation of approximately 900 acres of 
industrial zoned land. Among the parcels ofland involved in the potential annexation is 160 acres 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 8 l000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-16-05-1 
Page No. 2 
of fann land that is owned by a trust. The trust is controlled by three siblings, one of whom is the 
spouse of the councilmember's wife's cousin. You wish to know whether the councilmember is 
pennitted under the Act to participate in discussions by the city council regarding the proposed 
annexation and whether he may vote on actions to annex, rezone, or improve the property. You also 
wish to know whether the councilmember must disclose infonnation to the city council and the 
public about who owns the property. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
Section 87103 defines interest from which a conflict of interest may arise and includes the financial 
interest of a public official's immediate family. Immediate family includes the public official's 
spouse and dependent children. (Section 82029.) 
Based on the facts provided, Councilmember Belluomini does not have a financial interest 
in the potential annexation. The parcel of land in question is owned by a trust that is controlled by a 
relative of the Councilmember's wife. Because a cousin in-law does not fall within definition of 
"immediate family," Councilmember Belluomini does not have a financial interest in the trust that 
owns the parcel in question.2 
Therefore, Councilman Belluomini does not have a conflict of interest in the decisions 
concerning the potential annexation. Accordingly, recusal and disclosure is not required of 
Councilmember Belluomini. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
By: 
LN:jgl 
2 You have not identified any other potential interests in the individual such as income or gifts received, or any 
business association, Thus, we do not analyze these potential interests that can form the basis for a conflict of interest.

QUESTION: Under the Act, may Councilmember Belluomini participate in discussions and decisions 
concerning the potential annexation and rezoning of a parcel of land that is owned by a trust 
controlled by a relative of the councilmember's wife?

CONCLUSION: Yes. Councilman Belluomini does not have a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act 
prohibiting him from participating in decisions concerning the potential annexation and rezoning.
